mid.advice: A very annoying matchup. You can't really hit a Q into her, if her W is up, so always look for something IF she wastes it. 9/10 Ahri's will play aggresive early, especially with Auto -> W -> Auto -> Electrocute Combo Lvl 1. Try to always be aware of her E cooldown, getting hit by charm early will chunk you down by a LOT. Do not stand in the minion wave, as she will always Q it, which gives her both a way to push the wave and harass you at the same time, make her choose for one or the other. If she ults onto you, W her to make her combo take a LOT longer and it gives you more room to dodge her spells.
mid.itemsAP: I love Roam'Gath into her, as you completely remove that early strength of hers by just... not lane-ing. So shurelyas -> deadmans -> FoN (order of deadmans/fon can change)
mid.itemsTank: Warmogs/Kaenic feels great into her. FoN in late also works well into her
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: She hard wins into you once she hits Lvl 6, try to trade her Lvl 1 (and lvl 2 if she goes E instead of W Lvl 2). Late game you outscale her on both AP/Tank, just keep her from roaming early. Try to always be just slightly out of range of her Q so she wastes energy and never get hit by her E. Akalis E does an insane amount of damage. Also, go Oracle Lens after first reset. (This matchup is harder on toplane due to lane being longer)
mid.itemsAP: Hourglass to not get blown up later / Horizon Focus for Haste and Reveal
mid.itemsTank: Bami Cinder to reveal her during smoke (build it into Hollow)
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Don't fight pre 6, you will hard lose every trade. Scale and keep him from roaming but don't overextend. If possible, ask jungle for an early gank, you snowball REALLY hard in that matchup. Early armor/boots makes this matchup a LOT better. Try to always avoid his Q, when he Q's through the wave. Akshan will typically Q through a wave to E on top of you (as E targets units with his passive on them, prefering champ).
mid.itemsAP: Hourglass for late game to deny potential revives
mid.itemsTank: Wardens Mail/Tabi
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: She's an easy matchup pre 6, all she does is look for a Q -> E -> Auto -> Electrocute Combo onto you. If you avoid that, she doesn't do anything pre 6. Always be aware of her W, as it can setup a lot of ganks on you. Lvl 6 is where this matchup gets a bit more tricky as she will either play extremely aggresive and look to "cage" you into her R or she's gonna perma-push every wave with R into Roam. Always keep either Q or W (W is a lot easier to hit there) to cancel her ult. She has to stand still during Q/E cast, so look for a Q during that time-frame. YOU WILL NOT GET A STACK BY EATING HER WHILE SHE HAS EGG UP.
mid.itemsAP: Swifties into Ludens is my usual build into her, you can also play Roam'Gath well into her
mid.itemsTank: FoN works great into her - Mercs/Swiftie depending on enemy team
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Most importantly, keep an eye on her stun. An easy way to do that, is to left click on her and look at her status bar consistently. Whenever she does have stun up, you have a hard time trading. She has to stand still during Q/W so another time-frame to look for a Q. Use Q/W if she is about to stun you. Treat her E as an Ahri W, she gets a ton of MS during it and can use it to easily run out of your Q. If her E is down, she has 0 safety outside of her stun. Whenever she goes for a Q on a minion, you can snipe her with a Q. Ulting tibbers gives you a minion stack but also a quick way of clearing him as he can become pretty annoying, cuz he clears waves really easily and let's her setup a lot of vision.
mid.itemsAP: Banshees removes her Flash + Ult combo completely and gives you enough MR to survive anything else of her.
mid.itemsTank: Mercs is really good into her + Kaenic removes any of her burst power.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Always cancel his Q with either your Q / W. Keep in mind that his E can't be canceled by your W, you need to his your Q to cancel his flight. This matchup is REALLY free before he gets rylais and even then, its really free in a direct 1v1. Rylais just makes him able to set up a kill on you with his jungle. Deny his stacks by cancelling his Q under his turret.
mid.itemsAP: Swifties work insanely well into him, as it gives you an easier time to run out of his E/Q and it negates rylais slow a ton. Other than that, anything works (i usually go roam'gath into him, as you are always stronger in the early fights)
mid.itemsTank: Swifties work insanely well into him, as it gives you an easier time to run out of his E/Q and it negates rylais slow a ton. FoN in the late-game removes his kill potentional on you completely pretty much
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Very unplayable early game but gets better as the game goes on. Try to always avoid her Q - E Combo, as it will burn 40% of your HP everytime she hits it. (even at Lvl 2). Never give her free passive procs (3-Hit passive) as she will gain a good amount of sustain through that (and it does damage, duh). Once she hits Lvl 6, she will always try to play with her jungle and either gank mid or roam, always keep an eye on her (put a ward into the middle of the lane to see if she leaves lane or hides behind the walls). Don't forget that her W is a dash + invisibility that RESETS ON KILLS (idk why but many people forget about that). Always W her whenever she ults into you, as you get more counterplay into her usual one-shot combo and it makes it very awkward for her to play afterwards. Your Q out-ranges her, so try to always play around that. This matchup is a bit easier in midlane, as the lane in shorter, so you have an easier time to run out of her ult.
mid.itemsAP: Early Lost Chapter to be able to poke her down, without her being able to poke back. Swifties/Ionian Boots help a lot aswell, as you get to avoid her a lot more
mid.itemsTank: Early negatron, otherwise you quite literally can't lane
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Pretty much the same as asol, just a bit harder once he hits his item power-spikes (Nashors/Liandrys). He has to stand still during his W Autos + Q so you can always hit a Q into him during that time-frame. Try to not stand in the wave, as he can hit both you and the minion wave at the same time with his W. The matchup becomes kinda annoying once he hits Lvl 6, as he can always set up a kill on you whenever his jungle is mid/he has ult up. Also, do not push to far up once he reaches 6, as he can also shuffle you into the turret and look for a Solo-Kill on you. You can't really avoid is ult, outside of using W on him whenever he tries to shuffle you. He does out-scale you pretty hard once the game gets into 4-5 items late-game, but you should always be ahead before that even happens.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
mid.itemsTank: Because he basically only has DPS, no real burst, FoN is insane into him. Kaenic also works well to remove his poke.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Annoying matchup. Go AP into her if possible. Really important, avoid trading early IF she hits Q. Go 3 Points Q into W max. She outscales you but in late game, you can disable her REALLY well with Q/W. Boots early is quite important into her, as she will run you down if you do not have them. Go swifties if she has rylais. This matchup is a lot easier on mid, as she can't run you down as easily as in top.
mid.itemsAP: Early Swifties. Horizon Focus for Haste to lock her down more often
mid.itemsTank: Early MR into Boots. Force of Nature removes A LOT of her potential kill prio.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: It's just a really annoying matchup. Do not fight him before Lvl 6, treat him like an Akshan. He will always out-trade you, but you will out-scale him insanely hard. This is an insanely snowbally lane for both sides, if you die, he gets to lane/scale for free, same applies for the other way around. I always ask my jungle to gank him early, as his W has a long cooldown, so when he uses it, he's basically a free kill into free snowball. Keep in mind, that he does a ton of true-damage through his passive, lots of AP damage through his W/Q and has free armor shred on his E. Basically a lot of anti-tank in his kit. Also, they buffed his W damage by a lot in Patch 14.20, so avoid standing on top of it.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
mid.itemsTank: Early chain-vest makes his all-in a lot less deadly, look into HP after that (Warmogs works really well here). Deadmans in late gives you insane gap-close potentional as he doesn't have much mobility.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: If you play AP, you will have a really hard time into her. Her Shield is enough to negate any way of trading against her. This matchup is a lot easier on tank. Keep an eye on her runes, if she plays electrocute she wants REALLY fast trades (She will Q -> E -> W -> Auto). If she has conq, she wants longer trades (same combo just less burst and she wants to stay on top of you for longer). Its kinda hard, but if you are able to learn how to predict her E with your Q, this matchup gets a lot easier. You out-scale her as tank cho quite a bit, she basically can't kill you late. Diana's love roaming aswell, always spam-ping whenever she isn't in lane, otherwise she gets to snowball and she does that REALLY well. If you see her miss Q and she still E's, she doesnt get a reset, so a lot of her power is down during that time and you can try to go for a trade during that time. Her W is also a very long cooldown, so if she uses it, theres another time-window to trade. A well-timed W shuts her down completely in team-fights.
mid.itemsAP: Banshees/Hourglass works well into her, as she can't just all-in one shot you.
mid.itemsTank: Early negatron completely removes her Solo-Kill Threat on you, great Heartsteel stacker after negatron.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Basically the same as diana. He's always looking for really fast trades with E -> Q -> Auto -> Passive -> Electrocute. It's pretty unlaneable early, as you can't really trade into him. Only trade him whenever you have Grasp/HoB up. Keep in mind, he can dodge your W with his E2 (he teleports basically). Every Ekko always looks for a out-of-vision W to catch you off-guard (we Cho'gath mains do the same with our Q) so whenever he is not in lane, be aware that he might wait around a wall to W all in you. Always W him before you Q/R him, as he can't react with his ult then. In late-game teamfights, you can always look to "scare" him out of a full engage, if he does engage, W him and peel for your team. Tank is a lot better into him, keep in mind tho, that his W gives him max hp damage on every Auto whenever you are low on HP.
mid.itemsAP: Hourglass to not get hit by his W all-in late game. Roam'Gath works well into him aswell.
mid.itemsTank: Heartsteel - Kaenic feels really good into him (build some MR before you finish Heartsteel tho)
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Really easy matchup as tank, get's a bit harder on AP. He also plays very similar to Diana/Ekko, as he will always try to go for fast trades with Q -> Auto -> W -> Electrocute -> E out. Therefor, try to always stand out of his Q range. He has a really good all-in at Lvl 6 (especially with Ignite) so keep that in mind and do not stay in lane with low HP. W him while he is in Q animation so you can look for a trade/deny his trade during your silence.
mid.itemsAP: Banshees/Hourglass works well into her, as he can't just all-in one shot you.
mid.itemsTank: Heartsteel - Kaenic feels really good into him (build some MR before you finish Heartsteel tho)
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Really hard matchup, as he can't really be killed and out-trades you at pretty much any point in the lane-phase. Try to always keep your W for his W and a Q for his E. The longer he casts his W the more deadly it gets (longer duration on taunt, more damage and higher radius). Try to always play out-side of his Q range, as it does a LOT of damage due to it having max HP damage. Only trade whenever you have either HoB/Grasp up. When you are trading, keep his passive cooldown in mind, you wanna trade within 1 passive hit of his (5 sec flat cd, reduced by 3 seconds whenever he hits a champ/epic monster with an ability, this can only be reduced once per passive cd). Be sure to silence him during team-fights whenever he casts W/R, as you remove a lot of scary cc for your team. Always keep an eye on his item build, as he can build both AP or Tank (Just like Cho).
mid.itemsAP: I like RoA into him, as you do outscale him towards late game with it
mid.itemsTank: Early negatron into Heartsteel feels great. Abyssal in late makes him take a lot more damage than before.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Q him once he E's on you, quick trade afterwards, do not let him Q/W you after. You can't really kill him in lane if you play tank but you get a free lane phase to scale into. If you play AP, you can look to win every trade with HoB. Early Mercs is incredible into him, as tenacity also reduces his E CC and his Q Slow.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
mid.itemsTank: Mercs completely hard counter Gragas (his E cc + Q Slow gets reduced). If enemy team allows you to build Wits End aswell, it worked really well for me in the past.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Surprisingly hard in mid. You kind of can't trade into him, only with Q Poke + Comet. Try to hit both the minions and the turrets with ur Q. Only ask your jungle to gank, once you can get rid of his turrets, otherwise theres a good chance heimer will 1v2 you. Avoid getting hit by his E (the stun) at any point of the game, as his turrets will melt you during the stun duration (+ they will laser you). Treat his E like Ahri charm.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
mid.itemsTank: Early MR (Negatron) will decrease his lane-power by a lot as he can't build pen early. Force of Nature for late.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: He kinda perma pokes you without losing much mana. You are basically just tryna play for skirmishes with your jungle, as you won't really have any lane-kill-prio into him. Be aware that his W shield shields him more, the lower hp he is. He also perma regenerates his shield during the up-time of his ability. He will perma push wave but is very easy to gank, be sure to ping your jungle to gank mid, if he walks up. Once Hwei reaches Lvl 6, he can setup ganks pretty easily with his ult, so treat it kind of like a worse Azir setup. Try to always stay out of your minion-wave, as he will always QE (the fire wall) into EE (the little orianna ult thing) and will push wave + win trade against you (it will also proc passive on you). Always keep an eye on his passive, it doesnt look like it deals a lot of damage, but it adds up over-time.
mid.itemsAP: Early swifties, into anything depending on enemy. I always play Roam'Gath into him
mid.itemsTank: Warmogs feels great into him, go with some early MR (negatron or null-magic). FoN removes a lot of his damage but doesnt completely make him useless
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: You outscale hard in teamfight later on. Only trade her when there are barely any minons around/when she misses E, don't bother if that doesn't apply. Once she has BotrK, don't trade her if you do not have armor. As she will always push a wave since she jumps on them consistently, try to get your jungle to gank her. Irelia's really love overextending and if you can stabilize the lane, it gives you a lot of breathing room until she has BotrK. If you see her missing a Q reset on a minion, you have a quite long window to look for a trade, an irelia without Q/Stacks is a dead irelia. This matchup is a bit easier in mid, but still hard.
mid.itemsAP: Swiftie to dodge E/R is insane into her. Hourglass to stall her ult/dodge it
mid.itemsTank: Frozen Heart works great into her, as she scales well with Attack Speed.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Q him when he Q's you. Against Tank Jayce you win really hard, play to scale there. Lethality Jayce is a bit harder but as long as you avoid his all in with hammer Q, you win really hard. You also outscale him pretty well. Just do not let him snowball early, thats the most important part. Keep an eye out on his Eclipse Power-Spike as that one REALLY hurts Cho'Gath.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
mid.itemsTank: Great Heartsteel stacker. Early warden's shuts his early game down pretty hard. Deadmans to gap-close also works really well
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Typical Support Midlane, look out for whenever she has Mantra, as that is the only time she can trade really. She will always either RQ Poke or run at you with E - W and once ur rooted she will RQ. Hide behind minions, but not 2 close to them as her Q is AOE. Always look at her itembuild to kind of calculate how much damage she will deal. More karmas will decide to either go Malignance or Moonstone. She has really good gank setup with her W Root and has good sustain (is hard to kill), especially once she is low HP. Her RW will heal her more depending on low her HP is. She gains her ult back faster if she hits abilities, so try not to get hit unnecessarily.
mid.itemsAP: Swiftie first, afterwards anything works (Roam'Gath is my fav. here)
mid.itemsTank: Early Swiftie works well into her as you can run out of her W. Afterwards Warmogs feels great and for late game you can go Kaenic.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Quite literally the same as Kayle, you scale along side him and remove him from the late-game with 1 W. The only thing you have to be aware is his Lvl 6 Power-Spike as he can become quite annoying if you are not tank and he does a lot of damage early if he gets his W off. You can build Cull into him, if enemy jungle is also a lot more passive than aggresive (a Seju for example). Playing AP into him is *slightly* harder but still easy.
mid.itemsAP: RoA to scale along-side him or Roam'Gath (its really good into him)
mid.itemsTank: Free Heartsteel / Fimbul Game.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: The only way she becomes a threat is IF she goes Bork, but even then, as long as you kind of respect it and not just run blindly into her missing all spells, you win. She should never be able to even lane in the first place against you, as you both out-sustain, out-damage and out-scale her. This is probably the easiest matchup. Keep your W for her Ult and try to wait our her dagger until the last second to predict her E with your Q.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team (RoA is REALLY good into her, if she goes AP)
mid.itemsTank: Great heartsteel stacker (if she doesnt go bork), if she does go bork, drain-tank build (Fimbul-Unending) works really well into her.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Unloseable in early. You get free scaling and and still scale along-side her. Late-Game any Q/W against her kills her easily. If she goes swiftie, it makes it a bit harder to kill her, but she should never be able to kill you. Avoid dying to her at all costs, as she snowballs pretty well into you.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of her team
mid.itemsTank: Force of Nature late decreases her damage a lot, great heartsteel stacker.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Pretty easy matchup, DShield/Second Wind is giga strong here. Go Comet and Q Spam him, he doesnt have sustain. Whenever he E's in, W him so he can't combo you, be aware of his Electrocute. In late game, as long as you zone him out of teamfight / W him once he does somehow get through you, you win the game.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team (Mercs is goated into him tho)
mid.itemsTank: Mercs works great because of his perma cc. Late Game FoN is also really op into him as you can gap-close easier onto him + he has good amount of AP DPS
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: This matchup is basically just "suffer until you out-scale her". She will always out-trade you early, especially if she goes Electrocute. Always be aware of her Lvl 1 / 2 power, she can Auto W Auto you Lvl 1 for 250 ish damage. Lvl 2 it gets worse as she does  Auto - Q - W - Auto and will deal around 300 damage in 1 rotation. Her E / RE has insane gank-setup. Generally, you will lose lane, kind of like ambessa lane, but you hard out-scale her once you reach 2-3 Items. You can predict her W with your Q, its pretty easy to see whenever she will W into you. If you can't predict her W, W her instantly after she uses it so you deny her the free E.
mid.itemsAP: Lost Chapter/Catalyst into Mercs into Ludens/RoA is the go-to build
mid.itemsTank: Negatron / Mercs is must have early.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: The only way you die to her is if her jungle ganks her, she has INSANE setup for her jungle with a slow on her Q, a root on her W and a stun on her Ult. Especially on Lvl 6, treat it like a Malz/Azir lane, do not walk up blindly without knowing where the jungle is. Q her whenever she Q's and you won't be able to miss it. Whenever she wants to get out of a situation with her E, silence her and she can't reactivate it. Cleanse is hidden op into her, as you can cleanse her W and her Ult, which makes the lane a lot less deadly once she reaches Lvl 6. Outside of jungle ganks, shes really easy to beat. Mercs is also really good into her.
mid.itemsAP: Lost Chapter/Catalyst into Mercs into Ludens/RoA is the go-to build (Roam'Gath is really good into her aswell)
mid.itemsTank: Mercs is a must have, other than that, build towards entire enemy team
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Ultra boring lane, she kinda just pokes you out of lane (go second wind) and once she reaches Lvl 6, she perma wave clears with E - R. As long as you do not get hit by her Q consistently, you won't die in the lane but you will most likely not kill her either. I usually just roam away from her, as she is kinda stuck midlane at any point of the game. Mercs feels nice into her
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team (Mercs is good into her, i usually just go Roam'Gath and win for free)
mid.itemsTank: Mercs feel nice, but isnt needed. Anything works really
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Easiest matchup possible for Cho. You win every trade, you can't get poked out of lane and you win side later on. Play demolish so you can farm plates into him.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team (i love dark-seal into him)
mid.itemsTank: Really good heartsteel stacker + Fimbulwinter is free into him
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Actually quite hard, if he is good, due to his passive shield / item build / gank setup. He will always build liandry / rylais which hard counters you. Second Wind is really good into him. Always clear his W monsters, as it basically cuts his wave-clear in half. Keep in mind, that if you are E'd by him and he hits you with his Q or R, the E duration refreshes so try not to get hit by those abilities while afflicted by his E.
mid.itemsAP: I usually look into RoA into Tank or Roam'Gath
mid.itemsTank: Kaenic early - FoN late - removes any damage he does.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Only played into her once (as of 29.01.2025) and it felt alright (humanoid played her into me and i won). As long as you avoid her E, she won't have any kill pressure on you. Always W before you Ult as otherwise she will just W and completely remove your ult damage from the game. Don't let her stack her passive during lane by letting her perma attack you. She does become annoying in late but i wouldnt know of a advice against it yet, need to play into her more.
mid.itemsAP: Roam'Gath and she can't play the game, her waveclear isnt good enough to contest it
mid.itemsTank: FoN is good into her late, Warmogs feels good early into her aswell.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: This is one of the easiest assassin matchup (it got a little bit harder with the rework, as Naafiri can W your Q pretty consistently), tied with katarina. She can't really ever kill you and if she ever engages on you with her R, you just Q on top of yourself and trade quickly with HoB/Grasp. The only thing you have to watch out for is her roams, so spam ping whenever she isn't in lane.
mid.itemsAP: RoA into Tank or Ludens into full AP, late game Hourglass
mid.itemsTank: Heartsteel - Tear is free into her.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Very unplayable in lane phase but very easy in late. She will perma poke you with E - Q. Try to always avoid your own minion wave, as her E becomes a lot stronger if she hits a Unit before she hits you with it. Always keep an eye on her minion wave, to spot her, incase she hides "among them" ඞ. Spam ping your lane is missing once she isnt in lane, neeko's love to gank other lanes by hiding in their wave. Once she has Rocketbelt, she can combo that with her ult, keep that in mind.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works but i mostly try to avoid lane with Roam'Gath
mid.itemsTank: Negatron / Mercs is must have early.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Always look at her runes. If she goes Phaserush, she will be very passive early and hard to catch in the middle/late game, if she goes Aery/Comet, she will always try to hard-poke you out of lane early. Always keep her ball position in mind, she will try to put it in the middle of the wave most of the time. While she is ulting, you can hit her with a free Q to remove any combo potentional of her afterwards. If you do hit a Q into her, trading her feels really good, as she has the lowest base armor in the entire game.
mid.itemsAP: Ludens/RoA feels nice
mid.itemsTank: Warmogs early and FoN late feels good into her.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Outside of her good gank-setup with E + Blue Q and her Lvl 6 power-spike, this matchup is really easy. As long as you don't die, you hard outscale her. Whenever she presses E on you, Q instantly and she can't trade into you, this also applies to your W. Buy early armor and she can't kill you.
mid.itemsAP: Early Armor into RoA feels incredible here
mid.itemsTank: Chainvest or Wardens early into Heartsteel and she can't play the game.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Keep in mind that his Q will still be active if he used it before your Q/W, it does not get cancelled through CC. Always have an eye on his overheat bar, as he will ALWAYS win a trade if he his about to / overheating. Dodge his E at all costs, as it will reduce your magic resist by a LOT (honestly really broken ability). This matchup is easier in mid, as the lane is shorter therefor he can't freely run you down.
mid.itemsAP: Swiftie early into normal Ludens poke build is the usual for me
mid.itemsTank: Early MR (Negatron) to not get one shot, finish Negatron into FoN as secondary item.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Very simple matchup, he uses spell, you use Q. You out-trade and scale along-side him. You will win any early skirmish, as Cho is SO much better in those than Ryze. He does out-scale you once you hit full item build but most games do not get to that in the first place. Only thing to watch out for is his E - W Gank-Setup.
mid.itemsAP: RoA if you wanna scale along-side him, Ludens for better early skirmishing or Roam'Gath to just completely remove his team from the game as he can't roam well as Ryze
mid.itemsTank: Negatron into Warmogs. Late Game FoN or Kaenic depending on rest of enemy. Mercs feels good into him but isnt a must have, swifties work great aswell.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Whenever he uses either E / W, you can easily hit a Q on him, as he can't move during the cast-time for a good amount of time. The most important thing in this matchup is quite simple actually: DODGE HIS E. If he hits his E, he can follow up with W aswell, which increases his damage by a TON + lets him proc electrocute and gives him free stacks (12 HP per stack) + it gives him lane-sustain, as he heals through those stacks aswell. Keep in mind, that his E cooldown is reduced by 20% during his ult. Always try to CC him at the start of his ult and then disengage and let it run out. You won't win a direct 1v1 while he is in ult. This matchup is easier in mid, as the lane is shorter and you will have an easier time running out of his R.
mid.itemsAP: Swifties to be able to dodge his E a lot better + run out of his Rylais Ult combo easier.
mid.itemsTank: Force of Nature will reduce his damage by a lot. Mercs also feel really good into him as he has so much CC with his E Snare/W & R slow
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: One of the most infamous Cho'Gath matchups in both mid/top. This matchup is a bit worse in toplane, as the lane is longer, therefore he has an easier time to run you down. If you go AP, this matchup becomes a lot easier, as you will always be able to out-trade him before Lvl 6. Just Q on top of yourself, whenever he uses E on you, then combo him with HoB E and silence him so he can't combo you back, its really simple actually. If you play tank into him, you might suffer a lot more early-game, as you are missing the damage to kill him. Sylas loves long fights with his W Spam + Conq, avoid long fights against him, try to only do short trades with Grasp. You will HARD out-scale him on tank and you are 20x more useful in teamfights, just Q him whenever he E's anyone and W him whenever he is in range and he can't play the game.
mid.itemsAP: If he goes RoA, also build RoA to scale along-side him.
mid.itemsTank: Anti-Healing is pretty good into him, but you do not need it early. Build it after first item. He is a really good heartsteel stacker, its pretty free into him. Drain-Tank build is really good here
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: My usual perma ban. She out-scales you, can't be traded against (because she doesnt have to stand still while casting spells, therefore you can't really hit Q into her), her E completely removes any trade you could put out outside of Q Spam. 9/10 times she will go First Strike and farm gold on you while also stacking her passive on you for free. She has true damage and an execute once she reaches a certain amount of stacks (60 for true damage on W and 100 for R Execute). Her biggest power-spike will happen, once she upgrades her Q (40 Stacks) and she gets 2 charges on it. Always keep an eye on her balls, as she can position herself in front of a ball in a way, where it would hit you without her having to Q / E. Ask your jungle for early ganks, as she will always push the wave and try to harass you under turret.
mid.itemsAP: Nothing works, ban it (on a serious note, go swifties and Roam'Gath)
mid.itemsTank: Mercs / Negatron so she can't burst you early. Kaenic in late.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Pretty straight forward, Q her whenever she uses Q / W. Try to never get hit by her W, as her damage without it is pretty low. Her "ground Q" has a huge AOE and a good amount of damage, avoid getting hit by it by hiding behind minions. Her power-spike is at Lvl 9 when she puts 5 Points into her Q, so she's really weak before that. Your W doesn't cancel her Q so don't get tricked into that. Always keep her ult in mind, she can "gank" you with her jungle and just completely cut-off any escape-route.
mid.itemsAP: Roam'Gath into her is insane, as you both perma clear wave anyway and you can answer any of her ult/passive roams
mid.itemsTank: FoN cuts her Q damage in half, other than that.. look at enemy team and build towards them.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Respect his Lvl 1/2 and you won't ever lose lane. Always keep an eye on his passive stacks, as thats his entire damage early. Its really easy to predict his Q with ur Q. Talon will most likely just look to perma push wave into Roam, spam ping when that happens. Look at his runes when the game starts, if he has Conq, he wants to look for longer trades - the opposite applies if he goes Electrocute. W him whenever he is invisible, so he can't combo you/your team.
mid.itemsAP: I usually look into RoA into Tank with early Armor
mid.itemsTank: Chainvest / Wardens and he is out of lane.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: He basically just pushes lane and roams, as he can't kill you through your passive sustain. Look for early mercs / tenacity in runes. Q him whenever he W auto's you / Q's you. Whenever he reaches Lvl 2/3, he has E. Left click him to see his E stacks on his status bar (just like Annie passive), his E actually hurts a lot in the early game.
mid.itemsAP: Mercs into RoA or Roam'Gath to contest his roams
mid.itemsTank: Mercs and Kaenic late, he won't deal any damage.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: As long as you respect his E, this is one of the easiest matchups. The only thing to look out for is his gank-setup with his E. Whenever he uses ANY spell, you can hit him with a free Q which will force him to recall lots of times during lane. If he does hit E onto you, try to not get hit by the edge of it and W him whenever he gets 2 close. Try to not get hit by both his Q, while he aims for your minions. Just try to out-roam him.
mid.itemsAP: I usually look into RoA into Tank or Roam'Gath
mid.itemsTank: Mercs/Kaenic is rly good into him.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Please do not play tank into him, just play Roam'Gath and win the game through your other lanes. The lane-phase is really hard in general, as he will push faster than you and he out-ranges your Q range. A good vel'koz feels impossible. Try to always avoid getting hit by his passive. Once he hits Lvl 6, always keep either your Q or your W up to cancel his ult, otherwise he will one shot you. Hide inbetween minions to not get hit by his Q, but also don't get baited by his Q into an E from him, it's a weird back and forth.
mid.itemsAP: Roam'Gath.
mid.itemsTank: Early negatron into Kaenic.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Almost the same as Lissandra, Vex just has higher range and lower CC. Try to always dodge her E and respect her passive. While she casts either Q or E, you can hit a free Q on her. Once she ults you, Q on top of yourself once you see her reactivating it. She doesn't deal enough damage (if you respect her E + Passive) to push you out of lane by herself. Look out for her gank-setup once she reaches Lvl 6. Outside of that, its a free lane. You also hard out-scale her.
mid.itemsAP: Ludens or Roam'Gath usually. Banshees/Hourglass is also really good late, as it removes her entire engage.
mid.itemsTank: Negatron makes her not deal damage in lane - Build it into Kaenic late.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: Now imagine Syndra but with more movement and less burst damage, then you have viktor. He is pretty much untradeable against, because his Q shield is 2 big + every viktor goes shieldbash/boneplating combo. He outscales you really hard while also having really good upgrades against Cho (His Q upgrade is prob the best, as it makes it almost impossible to hit Q into him). His biggest power-spike will be his first upgrade, which will be his E upgrade, as he perma pushes waves with it and just scales for free while you can just watch it happen. Try to bait out his E by walking in and out of his range and "baiting" him into using it, otherwise its undodgeable. Don't try to trade while he has Q up. Play with bushes on sidelane after lane-phase and try to roam early.
mid.itemsAP: Roam'Gath is the only thing that feels playable into him, Swifties feel really good into him as it makes his W upgrade less effective + helps with running out of his W.
mid.itemsTank: Swifties feel really good into him as it makes his W upgrade less effective + helps with running out of his W. FoN for later teamfights and to run away/at him easier.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: A good vladimir is really hard to play against. Try to always W his E and during your W silence, try to hit a Q into him. Play towards your passive sustain, to sustain along-side him. If you play tank, there is little to nothing to do in lane, as you won't be able to kill him and he won't be able to kill you. If you play AP, play around vision and try to hit a Q from out of Fog of War and quick-combo him afterwards.
mid.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team. Oblivion Orb around Lvl 8/9 feels good into him.
mid.itemsTank: I love warmogs into him. FoN shuts down his kill-potentional completely and let's you run at him / away from him. Don't bother with bramble vest, he won't auto you anyways, once you have it
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Go swifties and he has a REALLY hard time against you. Whenever he charges up Q, you get a free Q (kind of like Asol). He also has to stand still for a long time during his W/E animation, so another 2 free Q windows (Don't think i have to mention his ult self-stunning him). He is also really easily gank-able as you can manipulate him into pushing the wave by getting hit on purpose while hiding in your own minion wave. Once he is pushed up, any jungle gank results in a kill.
mid.itemsAP: Swifties early into either Ludens or Roam'Gath
mid.itemsTank: Swifties into Kaenic, as kaenic will remove his poke (which is the only thing Xerath does)
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

mid.advice: In this matchup you can win short trades, but be careful with extended trades. Try to proc his passive with your E through minions and then trade. You can Q under yourself to predict his r as this will proc his passive which will expire while hes in the air. Don't let him stack his conq/lethal tempo ever, he will win a fight through that. Keep in mind that his Windwall can block your E Spikes. This matchup gets a lot easier in mid, as the lane is shorter and he can't run you down as easily.
mid.itemsAP: Hourglass for later but besides that anything works.
mid.itemsTank: Build armor vs yasuo as he will melt you with BotRK if you just build HP. Sunfire, bramble and Frozen Heart are great options. Randuins later is also good especially if they have other crit champs.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Actually playable, as in this matchup you can take short trades early, as long as you're able to disengage again. try to space his W and trade if you hit your Q. The trick is to predict his Q3 with your Q. W him when he goes in with E. Laning becomes very hard after he gets BotRK. Cho W "extends" the maximum E Time of Yone, so he can get baited into bad fights/dives, if you use it correctly (he can't go back himself/get forced back, but be aware, that he *could* get more damage in, during that time-frame). This matchup gets a lot easier in mid, as the lane is shorter and he can't run you down as easily.
mid.itemsAP: Hourglass for later but besides that anything works.
mid.itemsTank: Build armor vs Yone as he will melt you completely if you build hp. Both bramble, sunfire and frozen heart are great options. Building a little bit of MR can be good as he deals some ap damage, especially if enemy jungler is also ap. Randuins later is also good especially if they have other crit champs.
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: Honestly, the hardest Assassin Matchup. His passive really hurts, as it does Max HP damage and he can just completely ignore the lane by Q Spamming the minion-wave under turret. His Level 6 power-spike is as good as Qiyana/Fizz, if not better so be aware of that. Lot of people don't know that Zed E (if hit by himself, not his shadow) reduces his W cooldown. be aware of that. You can't really dodge his Q's very well, so let him waste it on minions instead. His biggest power-spike outside of Level 6 is his first item power-spike which will 9/10 times be eclipse. Once he has eclipse, he becomes REALLY hard to fight and trade against.
mid.itemsAP: Hourglass to remove his ability to ult you
mid.itemsTank: Great heartsteel stacker and also hourglass later (i also like Fimbul into him)
mid.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

mid.advice: You can't dodge her E consistent enough + you can't really lane into her. It's kind of like Syndra. She feels incredibly impossible to play against. Q/W her during her E Animation  and if she ults towards you, Q her original location in order to hit her on the way back. I usually just avoid lane-ing into her by playing Roam'Gath and just push wave into roam. If you can get your jungle to gank you, as she will always push/overextend, you can pick her off pretty easily. Cleanse works well into her, as you can cleanse her E but i usually still prefer TP/Ghost
mid.itemsAP: Swiftie to dodge her E (with a lot of luck) and Hourglass/Banshee later to avoid the E in the first place. I always play Roam'Gath into her.
mid.itemsTank: Kaenic.
mid.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: If possible, go tank into Aatrox. 2 Points in Q early to win fights once he steps up with E+Q. You do not win early against him but you can contest him early, try to scale with Grasp Autos, once you hit 3 Items, you outscale him. 1 W in the late game and he dies. Of course, Anti-Healing works great into him.
top.itemsAP: Oblivion Orb once he reaches Lvl 6
top.itemsTank: Bramble Vest once he reaches Lvl 6
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: She hard wins into you once she hits Lvl 6, try to trade her Lvl 1 (and lvl 2 if she goes E instead of W Lvl 2). Late game you outscale her on both AP/Tank, just keep her from roaming early. Try to always be just slightly out of range of her Q so she wastes energy and never get hit by her E. Akalis E does an insane amount of damage. Also, go Oracle Lens after first reset. This matchup is harder on toplane due to lane being longer.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass to not get blown up later / Horizon Focus for Haste and Reveal
top.itemsTank: Bami Cinder to reveal her during smoke (build it into Hollow)
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Don't fight pre 6, you will hard lose every trade. Scale and keep him from roaming but don't overextend. If possible, ask jungle for an early gank, you snowball REALLY hard in that matchup. Early armor/boots makes this matchup a LOT better. Try to always avoid his Q, when he Q's through the wave. Akshan will typically Q through a wave to E on top of you (as E targets units with his passive on them, prefering champ).
top.itemsAP: Hourglass for late game to deny potential revives
top.itemsTank: Wardens Mail/Tabi
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: In a direct 1v1, shes basically unbeatable before you stack loads of armor and even then, if she builds correctly, she still hard beats you. Don't waste your Q into her, when she has both her Q/E up, those dashes are 2 fast for you to predict it properly. Always stand near your team-mates, so you can counter her ult with your Q/W. Q yourself, whenever she ults you, you probably won't be able to dodge it, once you have a couple of stacks. In late-game teamfights, your W completely disables her for the silence duration, use that to hard counter her in those situations. This is basically just a "suffer hard in lane, win late game team-fights" kinda matchup.
top.itemsAP: Both Hourglass and banshee works great into her. Banshee removes her ability to randomly ult you and catch you, hourglass stalls her after ult.
top.itemsTank: Anti-Healing once she reaches 6, she heals a surprisingly high amount with her R passive + Conq.
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Very unplayable early game but gets better as the game goes on. Try to always avoid her Q - E Combo, as it will burn 40% of your HP everytime she hits it. (even at Lvl 2). Never give her free passive procs (3-Hit passive) as she will gain a good amount of sustain through that (and it does damage, duh). Once she hits Lvl 6, she will always try to play with her jungle and either gank top or roam, always keep an eye on her (put a ward into the middle of the lane to see if she leaves lane or hides behind walls/in the bushes). Don't forget that her W is a dash + invisibility that RESETS ON KILLS (idk why but many people forget about that). Always W her whenever she ults into you, as you get more counterplay into her usual one-shot combo and it makes it very awkward for her to play afterwards. Your Q out-ranges her, so try to always play around that.
top.itemsAP: Early Lost Chapter to be able to poke her down, without her being able to poke back. Swifties/Ionian Boots help a lot aswell, as you get to avoid her a lot more
top.itemsTank: Early negatron, otherwise you quite literally can't lane
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Really easy matchup until she has Trinity Force, everytime she E's onto you, Q yourself and then trade her until she has Q2, back off before she hits you with that. The second she has trinity, it becomes a bit harder, but still winable if you time ur abilities correct to not let her hit Q2 frequently. Try to bait out her E, once your jungle is around, as she has no escape afterwards. Try to always avoid getting hit by her Outer-Circle of her W, as it gives her lane-sustain. Early boots really help with Camille.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass to stall out ult in later teamfights
top.itemsTank: If you wanna get lane prio for grubbs/jungle, go Bami. If she has Conq, go anti-healing around Lvl 9. Also, free Heartsteel procs.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Annoying matchup. Go AP into her if possible. Really important, avoid trading early IF she hits Q. Go 3 Points Q into W max. She outscales you but in late game, you can disable her REALLY well with Q/W. Boots early is quite important into her, as she will run you down if you do not have them. Go swifties if she has rylais.
top.itemsAP: Early Swifties. Horizon Focus for Haste to lock her down more often
top.itemsTank: Early MR into Boots. Force of Nature removes A LOT of her potential kill prio.
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: One of the hardest matchups. You WILL lose any trade throughout the game. Don't overextend, he will run you down with Ghost. Whenever he E's you, he has to stand still, try to Q at the same time to avoid further damage. Go swiftie into him. Most important thing is not to die, try to scale alongside him.
top.itemsAP: Swifties to avoid perma dying to him. Hourglass to deny his ult for a bit longer in teamfights and to not get completely one shot
top.itemsTank: Swifties on first recall. Bramble Vest on second recall.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Honestly, I haven’t played against him much, but from what I know, the key is to avoid his Q poke in the early and late stages. Try to consistently hit him with your Q to remove his passive, making him easier to gank for your jungle. Anti-heal becomes really effective once he hits level 6, so keep that in mind. Going boots early is also a good idea to avoid getting stat-checked by him once he reaches that Lvl 6 Power-Spike.
top.itemsAP: Swifties on first recall if you have less than 1.2k gold, if you have 1.2k go lost chapter. Oblivion Orb once you finished Lost Chapter/Swiftie
top.itemsTank: Swifties on first recall. Bramble Vest on second recall.
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: One of the hardest matchups easily, shes designed to counter champs like Cho'Gath. Never Q before you W, she will parry it and use it against you, always W into Q. Walk against a wall whenever you have a fiora passive on you, if she can't proc it consistently, her damage decreases by a LOT. Perma ban this champ.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass to not get completely one shot late + to stall out ult.
top.itemsTank: Bramble Vest first recall. Can buy Hourglass late, if you need the AP aswell.
top.abilityOrderAP: W - Q - R - E

top.advice: Auto+E his Barrels and you win, avoid trading into his passive and call out his R, once he has it for your team. Always keep his W cooldown in mind, as he can easily deny ult-stacks with a well-timed W. He will always try to cheese the lane early, don't just stand under turret afk, look where he is setting up. Once he has Sheen, his lane-power increases by A LOT, don't let him perma poke you for free, Q him whenever he is in Q animation.
top.itemsAP: Swiftie to run out of barrel spam
top.itemsTank: Swiftie to run out of barrel spam, Heartsteel works really well into him.
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Always keep his R damage in mind, it does up to 2,2k Damage in late with runes. To win early trade, Q him once he's in Q animation, afterwards quick trade with E and avoid his E damage. Keep in mind, that his W Tenacity also works on your silence, don't get baited by that. Go swiftie into him.
top.itemsAP: Swifties to be able to "kite" him. Hourglass to deny his ult for a bit longer in teamfights and to not get completely one shot
top.itemsTank: Swiftie/Deadmans to outspeed him. Can build Hourglass later
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: One of my least fav. matchups. Honestly, no real clue on how to win this lane ONCE he gets swifties. Try to go AP with Comet and try to survive lane. Once lane-phase is over, you can always cheese him out of vision whenever he is mini-gnar.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass to deny his ult cc lock in late game team fights
top.itemsTank: Swiftie/Deadmans to be able to run away/run at him.
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Q him once he E's on you, quick trade afterwards, do not let him Q/W you him after. You can't really kill him in lane if you play tank but you get a free lane phase to scale into. If you play AP, you can look to win every trade with HoB. Early Mercs is incredible into him, as tenacity also reduces his E CC and his Q Slow.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
top.itemsTank: Mercs completely hard counter Gragas (his E cc + Q Slow gets reduced). If enemy team allows you to build Wits End aswell, it worked really well for me in the past.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: You win early (if you go AP) and hard lose late with... anything. Once she hits 1 Item (prob Riftmaker) you can't fight her anymore. If you go AP, try to always play around vision against her, shes very squishy outside of her W. If you play tank, position yourself in a way where, if she goes on you and her W is on cooldown, your team could save you and won't get his by her AoE in the process. Go swifties into her and pray.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass to "waste" some time. Banshee to deny first ult slow. Swiftie to not die perma into her/makes her R slow less effective
top.itemsTank: Force of Nature/Kaenic does decrease her damage by a lot, she will still deal a ton to you tho.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Haven't really played against him in Top, surprisingly hard in mid tho. You kind of can't trade into him, only with Q Poke + Comet. Try to hit both the minions and the turrets with ur Q. Only ask your jungle to gank, once you can get rid of his turrets, otherwise theres a good chance heimer will 1v2 you. Avoid getting hit by his E (the stun) at any point of the game, as his turrets will melt you during the stun duration (+ they will laser you). Treat his E like Ahri charm.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
top.itemsTank: Early MR (Negatron) will decrease his lane-power by a lot as he can't build pen early. Force of Nature for late.
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Q her whenever she W on you. Whenever she hits you with E, W/Q her and run out of E range. Swiftie Boots is ur biggest friend here. In late team fights, you can easily disable her with ur CC. Also, kill her tentacles when you can, it gives you passive HP/Mana + decreases her lane power by a lot. Try to not let your jungle gank your lane once she his Lvl 6, she will most likely win a 1v2.
top.itemsAP: Swiftie to run out of E Range. Hourglass to deny ult in late
top.itemsTank: Tabi/Swiftie early. Wardens Mail also works really well into her.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: You outscale hard in teamfight later on. Only trade her when there are barely any minons around/when she misses E, don't bother if that doesn't apply. Once she has BotrK, don't trade her if you do not have armor. As she will always push a wave since she jumps on them consistently, try to get your jungle to gank her. Irelia's really love overextending and if you can stabilize the lane, it gives you a lot of breathing room until she has BotrK. If you see her missing a Q reset on a minion, you have a quite long window to look for a trade, an irelia without Q/Stacks is a dead irelia.
top.itemsAP: Swiftie to dodge E/R is insane into her. Hourglass to stall her ult/dodge it
top.itemsTank: Frozen Heart works great into her, as she scales well with Attack Speed.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Q yourself whenever he Q's you, you do not win before Lvl 6. You hard snowball this matchup IF you get to kill him once. Lvl 6 you should be stronger. Keep his passive stacks/R passive stacks in mind, as he will not trade before stacking both up. Most importantly, get used to his E cooldown. If he has no E, he hard loses EVERY trade. If he has E, he hard wins every trade if you miss Q.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
top.itemsTank: Frozen Heart works great into him, as he scales well with Attack Speed. Great Heartsteel stacker tho
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Q him when he Q's you. Against Tank Jayce you win really hard, play to scale there. Lethality Jayce is a bit harder but as long as you avoid his all in with hammer Q, you win really hard. You also outscale him pretty well. Just do not let him snowball early, thats the most important part. Keep an eye out on his Eclipse Power-Spike as that one REALLY hurts Cho'Gath.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
top.itemsTank: Great Heartsteel stacker. Early warden's shuts his early game down pretty hard. Deadmans to gap-close also works really well
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Unloseable in early. You get free scaling and and still scale along-side her. Late-Game any Q/W against her kills her easily. If she goes swiftie, it makes it a bit harder to kill her, but she should never be able to kill you. Avoid dying to her at all costs, as she snowballs pretty well into you.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of her team
top.itemsTank: Force of Nature late decreases her damage a lot, great heartsteel stacker.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Q him once he E's in, don't ever trade him when his W is up. Always keep his W Cooldown in mind. Try to always dodge his Mounted Q. He will always try to combo both his mounted E and mounted Q together, it is side-stepable tho (in early, not in late). Basically all of his damage is based around his Mounted Q / W.
top.itemsAP: RoA / Hourglass is my go to build here.
top.itemsTank: Great Heartsteel stacker, Wardens/Tabis are great early game options. Frozen Heart works great aswell, if enemy team has another attack speed based champ
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Let him waste W, don't Q him before he uses it. Once he ults in a teamfight, keep your focus on him (your ult will be almost in kill range, as he loses 50% hp). It's pretty easy early but gets harder once he reaches 1 Item.
top.itemsAP: Liandry/Riftmaker if you are going for more of a bruiser build, Hourglass to stall time in late when he ults you over walls
top.itemsTank: Heartsteel/Fimbul
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Pretty easy matchup, DShield/Second Wind is giga strong here. Go Comet and Q Spam him, he doesnt have sustain. Whenever he E's in, W him so he can't combo you, be aware of his Electrocute. In late game, as long as you zone him out of teamfight / W him once he does somehow get through you, you win the game.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team (Mercs is goated into him tho)
top.itemsTank: Mercs works great because of his perma cc. Late Game FoN is also really op into him as you can gap-close easier onto him + he has good amount of AP DPS
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Easiest matchup possible for Cho. You win every trade, you can't get poked out of lane and you win side later on. Play demolish so you can farm plates into him.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team (i love dark-seal into him)
top.itemsTank: Really good heartsteel stacker + Fimbulwinter is free into him
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Q on top of yourself whenever he W onto you. Take every trade you can, whenever your HoB/Grasp is up. He has a LOT of gank-setup, so try to always keep an eye on the enemy jungle.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team (i love dark-seal into him)
top.itemsTank: Really good heartsteel stacker + Fimbulwinter is free into him
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Whenever he casts E, Q him. Whenever he casts R, wait for the Realm to arrive and then Q him immediately. Always keep his W Shield Meter in mind, you won't be able to trade him, if it is full/almost full. Hourglass is really strong into him late, as you can "wait" out his R. Force of Nature makes him not be able to kill you pretty much.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass to stall ult later / Banshee so he can't insta ult you before procing it
top.itemsTank: Force of Nature.
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Unloseable in early before Lvl 6. You get free scaling and and still scale along-side him. Only difference to Kayle is, that he can hard stat-check you Lvl 6, so keep that in mind. As long as you do not die early into him, he can't do much as you disable him late.
top.itemsAP: Swiftie.
top.itemsTank: Swiftie/Mercs (Mercs only, if the enemy team has a lot of CC in general) Great Heartsteel stacker
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Avoid trading him early, you won't win any "long" trades. Take short trades, Q him when he runs at you and Auto E him once, don't overextend and get caught by his Q Spam. Focus him in late teamfights with ur ult, as otherwise, he will kill youre entire team, if youre team isnt hyper-ahead. Don't bother fighting him in his ult. Also important: THE LOWER HP OLAF IS -> THE STRONGER HE GETS.
top.itemsAP: Swifties are the most important item into him. Hourglass second to "wait" out his ult
top.itemsTank: Swifties are the most important item into him. Great Heartsteel stacker, deadmans also works really well into him, as you reduce his slows even fruther + you can out-run him most of the time
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: He outscales you, out-trades you + is just a better version of Cho. Try to time ur Q with the end of his W, silence him around the time he would R2. You can easily predict his E, so just Q urself whenever he does engage. Be careful of his full combo if you dont have MR. Easily the hardest Tank Matchup for Cho.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team (I usually look into Max Haste Build into him)
top.itemsTank: Great Heartsteel/Fimbul stacker. Building MR against ornn is good, as he deals mostly %hp magic damage. So if you build just hp he will melt you anyway.
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Q yourself whenever he uses W on you, afterwards short trade. Don't play extended trades. Don't let yourself get poked down by his Q, sarcrifice minions for HP. You hard outscale him as both Tank/AP so as long as you don't die, youll be fine. Keep an eye on his items, if he doesnt go Bork, his damage towards you gets a LOT more bearable. His eclipse power-spike is huge tho. If he does go Bork, he's very squishy, so if you can survive 1 combo and he misses E, he most likely dies.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass as 2nd Item and he basically canno't all in you.
top.itemsTank: Wardens Mail / Tabi is ur best friend here. If he doesn't go Bork, Heartsteel/Fimbul also works great into him
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Second hardest "Tank" Matchup, she will basically just Q/Passiv spam you out of lane. Try to not be in Q range permanently, as her lane-power decreases by a lot, if she doesnt get a Q hit, everytime she casts it. Most importantly: SILENCE HER ULT AND DO NOT STAND AGAINST WALLS OR YOU LOSE.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of her team (i normally rush boots into her)
top.itemsTank: Heartsteel/Fimbul works great into her, cuz she usually also goes Fimbul.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Easy matchup, Q her original location when she E's you. Don't waste Q, otherwise you're always gonna get out-traded. SPAM-PING EVERY TIME SHE IS NOT ON LANE AFTER 6 OR SHE WILL MURDER YOUR TEAM.
top.itemsAP: Early swifties, so she cannot run you down. Hourglass second.
top.itemsTank: Wardens/Tabi will shut down her kill-prio on you completely
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Very similar to the Riven/Quinn lane, just a lot harder in lane and he scales better than her into you. You still hard outscale him, but just not as hard as Riven. Whenever he W you, Q on top of yourself. He wins both short and long trades early, so keep that in mind. Buy armor early on, as armor hard counters anything he does. W him in late, whenever he engages on you/your team to deny a W Stunlock Combo.
top.runeTank: Grasp (Bone-Plating is really good into him)
top.itemsAP: RoA / Hourglass is my go to build here.
top.itemsTank: Wardens/Tabi will shut down his kill-prio on you completely, also really free Heartsteel matchup, if you are ahead
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Always keep an eye on his passive stacks, thats where his entire power lies. Getting used to his rotations / cooldowns will help you a lot. Always keep his W cooldown in mind, he can heal your entire R damage, if you don't kill him with it directly. AVOID BUSHES. He's relatively easy to hit q on when he jumps from bushes, as you can q under yourself and hit him if you time it right.
top.itemsAP: RoA / Hourglass is my go to build here.
top.itemsTank: Wardens/Tabi will shut down his kill-prio on you completely, also really free Heartsteel matchup, if you are ahead
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: You hard outscale her. She hard wins early. As long as you do not give her a kill in lane and you are even (or just a little bit down) in CS, you win. If she uses Q3 aggresively onto you, Q yourself. Late-Game she literally can't play the game because of your W. Have her E shield in mind when you wanna ult her.
top.runeTank: Grasp (Bone-Plating is really good into her)
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of her team
top.itemsTank: Wardens/Tabi will shut down her kill-prio on you completely, also really free Heartsteel matchup, if you are ahead
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Keep in mind that his Q will still be active if he used it before your Q/W, it does not get cancelled through CC. Always have an eye on his overheat bar, as he will ALWAYS win a trade if he his about to / overheating. Dodge his E at all costs, as it will reduce your magic resist by a LOT (honestly really broken ability).
top.itemsAP: Swiftie early into normal Ludens poke build is the usual for me
top.itemsTank: Early MR (Negatron) to not get one shot, finish Negatron into FoN as secondary item.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: It's a really hard matchup on top, but pretty easy in mid. He can't really run you down midlane and you just out-scale him. Always keep his W bar/cooldown in mind, a lot of the power-budget of Sett is in that spell. Position away from minions as his E will stun you, if he hits both a minion and you with it (has to be one on either side). You have to position in teamfights completely differently into Sett, as he will ult you into your team and your entire team will die then. Try to isolate him away from your team.
top.runeTank: Grasp (Bone-Plating is really good into him)
top.itemsAP: Early swifties after lost chapter makes this matchup a lot more bearable
top.itemsTank: Go heartsteel if he doesnt go BotrK. Tabi feels awesome into him, build boots pretty early but do not rush them as first item
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Always keep an eye on his sword, avoid getting hit by it, it will slow you and increase his next 3 Q Autos by a LOT of damage/attack speed. Shen is one of the few champs in the game with a taunt, don't get baited and taunted under his turret. Shen W completely blocks any auto-attack damage, but it does not block your E Spikes, keep that in mind. Go demolish into him, to punish him, once he ults his team and is not in your lane.
top.itemsAP: RoA to outscale him completely.
top.itemsTank: Early MR feels really good against him, as he does have a LOT of Magic Damage in his kit.
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Shouldnt kill you before he has Liandrys, pretty easy matchup in general. If he proxys, you get free scaling. Late-Game is a bit harder, as he has one of the strongest ults in the game once he reaches Lvl 11/16, but if you do get a Q on him, he almost always dies instantly. FoN is really strong into him.
top.itemsAP: Swiftie, if he goes Rylais. I usually like RoA into him.
top.itemsTank: Swiftie, if he goes Rylais. Force of Nature will decrease his damage by a lot in late.
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: If he goes AD, the matchup is really easy as you just Q him during his Q. If he goes Tank, it's a lot harder, be aware of his W damage (it does max hp). Try to always hold either Q or W for his Q. If he does ult you, Q on top of yourself. AD Sion will always look for an Minion E into R Combo, be aware of that. Q HIS PASSIVE AND HE IS USELESS IN IT. Always keep an eye on sidelane, he will always push sides and win the game through it. ALWAYS PLAY TP INTO HIM.
top.itemsAP: RoA to scale with him.
top.itemsTank: Great heartsteel stacker, early armor also really helps as he is almost fully based around AD Damage, besides his E and W
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Whenever he uses either E / W, you can easily hit a Q on him, as he can't move during the cast-time for a good amount of time. The most important thing in this matchup is quite simple actually: DODGE HIS E. If he hits his E, he can follow up with W aswell, which increases his damage by a TON + lets him proc electrocute and gives him free stacks (12 HP per stack) + it gives him lane-sustain, as he heals through those stacks aswell. Keep in mind, that his E cooldown is reduced by 20% during his ult. Always try to CC him at the start of his ult and then disengage and let it run out. You won't win a direct 1v1 while he is in ult.
top.itemsAP: Swifties to be able to dodge his E a lot better + run out of his Rylais Ult combo easier.
top.itemsTank: Force of Nature will reduce his damage by a lot. Mercs also feel really good into him as he has so much CC with his E Snare/W & R slow
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: One of the most infamous Cho'Gath matchups in both mid/top. This matchup is a bit worse in toplane, as the lane is longer, therefor he has an easier time to run you down. If you go AP, this matchup becomes a lot easier, as you will always be able to out-trade him before Lvl 6. Just Q on top of yourself, whenever he uses E on you, then combo him with HoB E and silence him so he can't combo you back, its really simple actually. If you play tank into him, you might suffer a lot more early-game, as you are missing the damage to kill him. Sylas loves long fights with his W Spam + Conq, avoid long fights against him, try to only do short trades with Grasp. You will HARD out-scale him on tank and you are 20x more useful in teamfights, just Q him whenever he E's anyone and W him whenever he is in range and he can't play the game.
top.itemsAP: If he goes RoA, also build RoA to scale along-side him.
top.itemsTank: Anti-Healing is pretty good into him, but you do not need it early. Build it after first item. He is a really good heartsteel stacker, its pretty free into him
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Probably the 3rd hardest tank matchup, as he will always out-damage you with his passive/Q while still having a good amount of sustain through his Q. Always stand behind minions, as you do not want to get poked down by his Q. Early swifties also make this matchup a lot easier, as you will be able to dodge his Q into W combo easier (it's pretty impossible to dodge if you do not have swifties). Always W into Ult, as he can just simply block your ult damage with his shield once he is low. Never overextend under his turret into him, he can easily just eat you and kid-nap you under turret. A lot of people don't know, that Tahm can Q + R you from range once you have 3 passive stacks on you, basically he can kid-nap you from range.
top.itemsAP: I usually build RoA into him to try to out-scale him late. Swifties early to be able to dodge his stuff more easily
top.itemsTank: Heartsteel/Abyssal Mask feels good into him. Mercs/Swifties based on preference (i like mercs more here)
top.abilityOrderAP: 2 Points Q - 2 Points E-  Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Pretty easy matchup before he gets swifties, DShield/Second Wind is giga strong here. Go Comet and Q Spam him, he doesnt have sustain. Q Max on both AP/Tank Cho. Get oracle once he hits Lvl 6, denying his shrooms removes a lot of safety from him. Teemo players will often AA+Q you, so try to hit your Q when he goes for that. Often you can extend trades a bit if you hit Q.
top.itemsAP: Swiftie early
top.itemsTank: Force of Nature will decrease his damage by a lot and you will be able to potentionally get on top of him.
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Avoid playing Tank into him. Go boots early to not let him run you down. Always keep his E cooldown in mind, he won't be able to get on top of you without it, once you have boots diff. He will always try to punish side-lanes, never leave him alone, he one-shots turrets (basically like a Sion but more annoying). Always try to either stall out his ult or wait until he uses ult on someone else. Trundle is pretty easy outside of anything mentioned above (which still means he is ultra hard lmao), as you have a really easy time locking him down with Q, outside of his W MS Boost, he doesnt really have a chance to dodge it.
top.itemsAP: Second Item Hourglass to "wait" out his ult. Early Swifties also makes this matchup a lot easier
top.itemsTank: If you need some AP, build Hourglass to "wait" out his ult. If he doesnt go Bork, great heartsteel stacker. Early Swifties also makes this matchup a lot easier
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: This matchup is basically the same as trundle, just that he has a dash but a lot less damage once you build armor. It's pretty easy to predict his dash with your Q, as he will just dash on-top of you in 99% of the times. Early armor makes this matchup very easy. Tryndamere loves long trades with lethal-tempo + passive crits + E spam, try to only trade if you have HoB/Grasp up. Tryndamere W kinda works like Cassio R.. just the other way around. If you look at him, you completely counter the slow of it (the AD reduction still applies regardless).
top.itemsAP: Hourglass to stall out his ult and get some much needed armor, Cosmic also feels great for the haste/ms
top.itemsTank: Randuins/Frozen heart is your best friend here. Tabis are also incredible into him. Build some armor and then go into heartsteel.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Don't ever trade him when he has his passive up, you will 100% lose every trade. If he does try to get on-top of you with his E, always Q right before he hits you, as he has to stand still a little bit longer during that auto animation, afterwards disengage. Always keep an eye on what he is building, as he can go both AP/AD. I hate this champ.
top.itemsAP: Swifties / Cosmic to not get completely run down by him
top.itemsTank: FoN if he goes AP, Bramble/Sunfire/Randuins if he goes AD (honestly i need to test if Randuins still hard counters AD Udyr)
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: This matchup is a lot easier when you go AP, as you out-range him pretty hard with your Q (it's still REALLY hard tho). I believe, he is easily one of the biggest Cho'Gath counters in the game for toplane. Let him waste his passive auto's on minions. Avoid getting hit / cheesed by his E at all costs, if you can't avoid it, Q on top of yourself to avoid any further one-shot damage. Keep in mind, that if he does not hit you with a Q/E, his W won't automatically target you (you can play around minion waves then and let them tank it).
top.itemsAP: Hourglass lets you stall out his ult in late
top.itemsTank: Wardens Mail / Bramble rush otherwise unplayable
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Kind of same as Vayne just 500% worse. Avoid dying in lane (it's a lot harder to not die into him in comparison to Vayne) and try to let him starve. He spikes REALLY hard on 1 Item, so keep an eye on that. Avoid any long trades, his W damage + PTA will always out-trade you. Whenever he does charge up his Q, try to either Q him (if he is far away) or instantly W him (if he isn't far away). Do not overextend when he has ult up, he pretty much one-shots you at Lvl 6.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass to avoid his R into WQ Combo. Early Lost-Chapter to make him starve easier, as you will have more mana to CS under turret with.
top.itemsTank: He has a lot of mixed damage, Jak'Sho 3rd item feels great into him. You can build Hourglass (especially if he goes AP, as he will one shot you if you do not build hourglass) to dodge his QW pop. Warmogs used to feel good into him, but idk about the new one
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: Do NOT die in lane. If she does play vayne top, just let her "starve". In 99% of the games, her team-comp suffers hard because of her pick, as long as she doesn't get to cheese. If you need to give up CS in order to not die, so be it. Get your jungle to help you, as she will always push the wave and is a pretty free matchup once you do get a lead on her (especially if you go AP). Of course, kinda like poppy, do not position yourself against a wall. Also, do not waste Q when she has Q up, she will always be able to dodge it.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of her team (i do like Horizon/Hourglass into her tho)
top.itemsTank: Deadmans feels awesome into her, as it helps you catch up/run away from her. Warmogs also felt great (idk about the new one tho)
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E

top.advice: A good vladimir is really hard to play against. Try to always W his E and during your W silence, try to hit a Q into him. Play towards your passive sustain, to sustain along-side him. If you play tank, there is little to nothing to do in lane, as you won't be able to kill him and he won't be able to kill you. If you play AP, play around vision and try to hit a Q from out of Fog of War and quick-combo him afterwards.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team. Oblivion Orb around Lvl 8/9 feels good into him.
top.itemsTank: I love warmogs into him. FoN shuts down his kill-potentional completely and let's you run at him / away from him. Don't bother with bramble vest, he won't auto you anyways, once you have it
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Always keep an eye on his cooldowns, he hard wins every trade if he hits you with Q/E Combo. Don't bother with long trades into him, you will never be able to out-damage his W healing. Look for short-trades whenever HoB/Grasp is up and his E is on cooldown. You hard out-scale him in teamfights, just keep an eye on sidelane and do not leave him alone there. Don't ever let him W you when he marked you, as thats how he heals and outsustains. Q yourself whenever he runs onto you and gets into Q animation, you completely remove any of his trade potential.
top.itemsAP: RoA if he goes RoA to scale along-side him, otherwise anything works.
top.itemsTank: He has a lot of mixed damage, Jak'Sho 3rd item feels great into him. He is a great heartsteel stacker.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Pretty much unplayable in early. You won't win any trades and even if you think you did, he heals of minions. Keep in mind, that warwicks healing gets stronger, the lower hp he is (kinda like Olaf). So a "almost dead" warwick.. isn't really almost dead. Low HP is warwicks comfort zone. Try to always Q after his Q, as his Q makes him unstoppable. Try to scale along-side him and you should be stronger in late-game teamfights. Warwick loses a lot of power, if he doesn't get to snowball.
top.itemsAP: Oblivion Orb rush.
top.itemsTank: Bramble rush.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Whenever he uses E on you, W him into Q. If you do it correctly, he can't insta W to dodge your Q. Otherwise, just trade whenever you have HoB/Grasp up. This matchup is pretty simple and he shouldn't really be able to kill you in lane, it's very similar to the jax matchup.
top.itemsAP: Anything works, look at the rest of his team
top.itemsTank: Early Wardens feels ultra good into him.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: In this matchup you can win short trades, but be careful with extended trades. Try to proc his passive with your E through minions and then trade. You can Q under yourself to predict his r as this will proc his passive which will expire while hes in the air. Don't let him stack his conq/lethal tempo ever, he will win a fight through that. Keep in mind that his Windwall can block your E Spikes.
top.itemsAP: Hourglass for later but besides that anything works.
top.itemsTank: Build armor vs yasuo as he will melt you with BotRK if you just build HP. Sunfire, bramble and Frozen Heart are great options. Randuins later is also good especially if they have other crit champs.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Actually playable, as in this matchup you can take short trades early, as long as youre able to disengage again. try to space his W and trade if you hit your Q. The trick is to predict his Q3 with your Q. W him when he goes in with E. Laning becomes very hard after he gets BotRK. Cho W "extends" the maximum E Time of Yone, so he can get baited into bad fights/dives, if you use it correctly (he can't go back himself/get forced back, but be aware, that he *could* get more damage in, during that time-frame).
top.itemsAP: Hourglass for later but besides that anything works.
top.itemsTank: Build armor vs Yone as he will melt you completely if you build hp. Both bramble, sunfire and frozen heart are great options. Building a little bit of MR can be good as he deals some ap damage, especially if enemy jungler is also ap. Randuins later is also good especially if they have other crit champs.
top.abilityOrderAP: 3 Points Q - Max W - Finish Q - R - E

top.advice: Hard matchup, his W is really hard to play around. Try to always kill his ghouls and use Auto + E to kill his cage. Ult his maiden everytime you get the chance to, as his power decreases drastically, once maiden is dead. Dodge E at all costs.
top.itemsAP: Swiftie.
top.itemsTank: Swiftie.
top.abilityOrderAP: Q - W - R - E
